plssem 0.1.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Categorical Models with plssem
    • Interaction Models with plssem
    • Linear Models with plssem
    • Multilevel Models with plssem
    • Ordinal Regression with plssem
    • Parallel Bootstrapping with plssem

Multilevel Models with plssem

Source: vignettes/multilevel-models.Rmd
multilevel-models.Rmd

This vignette shows examples of multilevel random slopes and intercept models, with both continuous and ordinal data.

Random Slopes Model 

slopes_model <- "
  X =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  Z =~ z1 + z2 + z3
  Y =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  W =~ w1 + w2 + w3
  Y ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
  W ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
"

Continuous Indicators 

fit_slopes_cont <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopes,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_slopes_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   127.210
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.008
#>   RMSEA                                          0.018
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.860
#>   x2                                             0.683
#>   x3                                             0.772
#>   z1                                             0.839
#>   z2                                             0.694
#>   z3                                             0.759
#>   y1                                             0.838
#>   y2                                             0.726
#>   y3                                             0.750
#>   w1                                             0.840
#>   w2                                             0.695
#>   w3                                             0.771
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.542
#>   W                                              0.553
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.927      0.008  116.333    0.000
#>     x2              0.826      0.012   68.323    0.000
#>     x3              0.879      0.008  105.033    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.916      0.008  108.665    0.000
#>     z2              0.833      0.010   83.862    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.009   93.988    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.915      0.012   78.906    0.000
#>     y2              0.852      0.012   73.043    0.000
#>     y3              0.866      0.011   76.167    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.916      0.011   83.246    0.000
#>     w2              0.834      0.013   62.371    0.000
#>     w3              0.878      0.012   71.041    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.293      0.019   15.136    0.000
#>     Z               0.444      0.024   18.561    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.393      0.036   10.936    0.000
#>     Z               0.248      0.043    5.747    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.010      0.004    2.180    0.029
#>    .W               0.010      0.008    1.239    0.216
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.175      0.016   11.281    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.004      0.005   -0.669    0.503
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.014   -1.773    0.076
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.009    1.296    0.195
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.003      0.012    0.251    0.802
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.011    0.881    0.378
#>     W~X             0.013      0.012    1.147    0.251
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.458      0.026   17.874    0.000
#>    .W               0.447      0.031   14.584    0.000
#>    .x1              0.140      0.015    9.439    0.000
#>    .x2              0.317      0.020   15.925    0.000
#>    .x3              0.228      0.015   15.459    0.000
#>    .z1              0.161      0.015   10.425    0.000
#>    .z2              0.306      0.017   18.507    0.000
#>    .z3              0.241      0.016   14.925    0.000
#>    .y1              0.162      0.021    7.644    0.000
#>    .y2              0.274      0.020   13.875    0.000
#>    .y3              0.250      0.020   12.733    0.000
#>    .w1              0.160      0.020    7.953    0.000
#>    .w2              0.305      0.022   13.774    0.000
#>    .w3              0.229      0.022   10.565    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.086      0.019    4.604    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.006    3.158    0.002
#>     Y~Z             0.105      0.015    6.909    0.000
#>     W~1             0.059      0.011    5.494    0.000
#>     W~X             0.095      0.014    6.909    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.144      0.025    5.749    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_slopes_ord <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopesOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomSlopesOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_slopes_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   263.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.010
#>   RMSEA                                          0.029
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.870
#>   x2                                             0.669
#>   x3                                             0.789
#>   z1                                             0.841
#>   z2                                             0.714
#>   z3                                             0.758
#>   y1                                             0.844
#>   y2                                             0.711
#>   y3                                             0.754
#>   w1                                             0.835
#>   w2                                             0.681
#>   w3                                             0.785
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.539
#>   W                                              0.550
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.933      0.013   71.972    0.000
#>     x2              0.818      0.012   66.122    0.000
#>     x3              0.888      0.012   74.518    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.917      0.014   63.509    0.000
#>     z2              0.845      0.015   54.691    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.012   70.238    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.918      0.012   79.337    0.000
#>     y2              0.843      0.012   68.738    0.000
#>     y3              0.869      0.014   64.085    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.914      0.011   80.678    0.000
#>     w2              0.825      0.017   49.958    0.000
#>     w3              0.886      0.017   50.862    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.290      0.022   13.354    0.000
#>     Z               0.451      0.037   12.274    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.391      0.042    9.215    0.000
#>     Z               0.245      0.058    4.258    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.011      0.005    2.209    0.027
#>    .W               0.009      0.007    1.339    0.181
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.168      0.014   12.221    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.007      0.005   -1.240    0.215
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.025      0.015   -1.675    0.094
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.010    1.199    0.231
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.004      0.009    0.401    0.688
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.010      0.012    0.780    0.436
#>     W~X             0.016      0.014    1.157    0.247
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.461      0.029   15.704    0.000
#>    .W               0.450      0.029   15.258    0.000
#>    .x1              0.130      0.024    5.367    0.000
#>    .x2              0.331      0.020   16.352    0.000
#>    .x3              0.211      0.021    9.962    0.000
#>    .z1              0.159      0.027    5.958    0.000
#>    .z2              0.286      0.026   10.980    0.000
#>    .z3              0.242      0.022   11.245    0.000
#>    .y1              0.156      0.021    7.342    0.000
#>    .y2              0.289      0.021   14.025    0.000
#>    .y3              0.246      0.023   10.484    0.000
#>    .w1              0.165      0.021    8.002    0.000
#>    .w2              0.319      0.027   11.741    0.000
#>    .w3              0.215      0.031    6.984    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.084      0.022    3.754    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.491    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.101      0.014    7.180    0.000
#>     W~1             0.058      0.014    4.250    0.000
#>     W~X             0.099      0.016    6.404    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.142      0.029    4.937    0.000

Random Intercepts Model 

intercepts_model <- '
  f =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  f ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + w1 + w2 + (1 | cluster)
'

Continuous Indicators 

fit_intercepts_cont <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomIntercepts,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_intercepts_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    24.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.891
#>   y2                                             0.785
#>   y3                                             0.814
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.744
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.944      0.008  123.181    0.000
#>     y2              0.886      0.010   85.745    0.000
#>     y3              0.902      0.008  106.805    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.238      0.007   34.390    0.000
#>     x2              0.162      0.007   24.375    0.000
#>     x3              0.077      0.006   12.366    0.000
#>     w1              0.128      0.033    3.882    0.000
#>     w2              0.091      0.038    2.376    0.017
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.013      0.008   -1.679    0.093
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.104      0.011    9.603    0.000
#>     x3              0.004      0.013    0.325    0.745
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.097      0.010    9.548    0.000
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.041      0.054   -0.760    0.447
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.256      0.016   16.523    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.109      0.014    7.515    0.000
#>    .y2              0.215      0.018   11.762    0.000
#>    .y3              0.186      0.015   12.182    0.000
#>     f~1             0.621      0.020   31.600    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_intercepts_ord <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomInterceptsOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomInterceptsOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_intercepts_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    25.508
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.885
#>   y2                                             0.788
#>   y3                                             0.809
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.723
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.941      0.012   81.362    0.000
#>     y2              0.888      0.011   82.258    0.000
#>     y3              0.899      0.010   86.570    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.241      0.008   30.398    0.000
#>     x2              0.158      0.006   25.882    0.000
#>     x3              0.080      0.007   11.810    0.000
#>     w1              0.121      0.043    2.820    0.005
#>     w2              0.081      0.038    2.116    0.034
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.012      0.009   -1.287    0.198
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.110      0.011   10.325    0.000
#>     x3              0.012      0.009    1.334    0.182
#>     w1              0.001      0.003    0.215    0.830
#>     w2              0.001      0.003    0.375    0.708
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.099      0.012    8.002    0.000
#>     w1             -0.003      0.003   -0.893    0.372
#>     w2             -0.001      0.003   -0.434    0.664
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1             -0.003      0.002   -1.156    0.248
#>     w2              0.003      0.003    0.975    0.329
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.025      0.062   -0.400    0.689
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.277      0.014   19.263    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.115      0.022    5.250    0.000
#>    .y2              0.212      0.019   11.084    0.000
#>    .y3              0.191      0.019   10.250    0.000
#>     f~1             0.601      0.018   33.052    0.000
